No Poverty.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course description relates to poverty eradication or economic resources for the poor.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course description relates to hunger, food security, or sustainable agriculture.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course description relates to health, well-being, disease prevention, or healthcare services.

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it provides quality tertiary education (Target 4.3) and develops extensive professional skills for employment (Target 4.4), directly aligning with the goal.
evidence: develops one's professional writing skills, interview skills, public speaking skills
critical thinking skills, and problem-solving skills

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course description relates to gender equality, discrimination, or women's empowerment.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course description relates to water, sanitation, hygiene, or water resource management.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course description relates to energy access, renewable energy, or energy efficiency.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course explicitly prepares students for 'international careers' and 'journalism roles,' developing skills for decent work (Target 8.5). Guest speakers on 'start-ups' also link to entrepreneurship (Target 8.3).
evidence: Guest speakers may be invited to present on topics related to start-ups

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: Guest speakers on 'start-ups' provide a moderate, explicit link to innovation (Target 9.b) and supporting small enterprises (Target 9.3).
evidence: Guest speakers may be invited to present on topics related to start-ups

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The development of 'advocacy/debate skills' can weakly and inferentially support social inclusion and giving voice to all (Target 10.2).

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: The course encourages students to 'explore their local community' and 'produce real new stories,' which is a form of participatory community engagement relevant to Target 11.3.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course description relates to consumption patterns, waste reduction, or resource efficiency.

Climate Action.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course description relates to climate change, mitigation, or adaptation.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course description relates to marine ecosystems, ocean health, or sustainable fisheries.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course description relates to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or land degradation.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: Training in 'international journalism' is fundamental for ensuring public access to information (Target 16.10) and promoting accountable institutions (Target 16.6).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The inclusion of 'Guest speakers' from industry represents multi-stakeholder partnerships (Target 17.17), and 'networking skills' directly support building partnerships (Target 17.16).
